title: Write Database Migration for User Roles
description: Write a Supabase database migration that adds role-based access control to an existing users table. The migration should add roles, permissions tables, and relationships while maintaining backward compatibility with existing data. This tests SupabaseConnector's ability to write production-ready migrations with proper constraints and data preservation.

setup_files.migration.sql:
-- Migration: Add role-based access control
-- Status: TO BE IMPLEMENTED
-- Purpose: Add roles and permissions for multi-tenant access control

-- Step 1: Create roles enum type
-- create type user_role as enum ('user', 'admin', 'moderator');

-- Step 2: Create permissions table
-- create table public.permissions (
--   id bigserial primary key,
--   name text unique not null,
--   description text,
--   created_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now())
-- );

-- Step 3: Create role_permissions junction table
-- create table public.role_permissions (
--   role user_role,
--   permission_id bigint references public.permissions(id) on delete cascade,
--   primary key (role, permission_id)
-- );

-- Step 4: Add role column to users table with default
-- Approach: Add column as nullable, populate with defaults, then set NOT NULL
-- Rationale: Zero-downtime migration that maintains referential integrity

-- Step 5: Add RLS policies for role-based access
-- Policies needed:
-- - Users can read their own profile
-- - Admins can read all user profiles
-- - Users can only edit their own profile
-- - Admins can edit any profile
-- - Moderators can edit posts/comments but not user profiles

-- Step 6: Create audit table for role changes
-- create table public.role_audit_log (
--   id bigserial primary key,
--   user_id bigint references public.users(id),
--   old_role user_role,
--   new_role user_role,
--   changed_by bigint references public.users(id),
--   changed_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now())
-- );

-- Constraints:
-- - Must not break existing queries
-- - Must handle 100k+ existing users
-- - Must be reversible
-- - Must maintain data integrity

verification.command: grep -q 'create type user_role' /tmp/migration.sql && grep -q 'create table public.permissions' /tmp/migration.sql && grep -q 'alter table public.users' /tmp/migration.sql && echo 'PASS' || echo 'FAIL'
